daily_brief: Diplomatic activity intensified ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara as President Trump held separate phone calls with Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelensky, with the Kremlin describing the nearly 90-minute Trump-Putin conversation as 'businesslike and constructive' [France 24, Al Jazeera, Le Monde]. Ukraine continued its strategic campaign against Russian energy infrastructure, striking a major oil terminal in St. Petersburg approximately 850km from its border, with Kyiv claiming nearly 43% of Russia's oil refining capacity has been disabled [BBC]. The United States marked its 250th anniversary with celebrations on the National Mall, though severe weather forced evacuations before Trump's evening address, during which he attacked domestic opponents as 'communists' [BBC, France 24, Al Jazeera]. In Iran, the delayed state funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed in February airstrikes, drew large crowds with some attendees calling for Trump's death [The Hindu]. Meanwhile, France awaits a consequential court verdict Tuesday that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in next year's presidential election, with polls showing her as the frontrunner [BBC].

top_stories.headline: Trump Holds Calls with Putin and Zelensky Ahead of Critical NATO Summit in Turkey
bullets: Trump spoke with Putin for nearly 90 minutes on Saturday, offering to help find a solution to end the Ukraine war, according to Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov
The Kremlin described the conversation as 'businesslike and quite constructive,' with Russia seeking a 'political-diplomatic resolution' accounting for its 'fundamental approach'
Trump also spoke with Zelensky, discussing the war's 1,200km front line; Zelensky stated 'American resolve will have a crucial meaning'
Negotiations remain at an impasse, with Moscow demanding withdrawal of Ukrainian forces from Donetsk region, which Kyiv rejects

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with both calls equally, emphasized NATO summit context

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused primarily on Trump-Putin call and Kremlin's characterization

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article available but content not fully accessible

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Covered in context of Ukraine drone strikes story

top_stories.headline: Ukraine Strikes Major Oil Terminal in St. Petersburg, Escalating Energy Infrastructure Campaign
bullets: Ukrainian drones hit a major oil terminal in St. Petersburg overnight, approximately 850km from Ukraine's border
Ukraine also struck a major Russian naval base in the region; St. Petersburg Governor acknowledged the 'massive' drone attack but reported no casualties
Kyiv claims nearly 43% of Russia's oil refining capacity has been 'disabled' by recent attacks, though this figure is unverified
Putin signed a new law Saturday aimed at boosting domestic fuel supplies after rare admission that Ukrainian attacks caused shortages

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage emphasizing strategic significance and Putin's domestic response

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Mentioned within liveblog coverage of diplomatic developments

top_stories.headline: US Celebrates 250th Anniversary as Trump Delivers Politically Charged Address After Storm Delays
bullets: Severe thunderstorms forced evacuation of tens of thousands from the National Mall, delaying celebrations by several hours
Trump's speech hailed America as the 'crowning achievement of human history' and declared the 'American dream is back'
The president attacked domestic opponents as 'communists' and touched on political themes including the Save America Act and right to bear arms
Critics accused Trump of making himself central to the milestone and politicizing the bicentennial celebrations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting both celebrations and criticism of politicization

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's anti-communist rhetoric and political nature of speech

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage focused on 'greatest force for peace' quote

top_stories.headline: Iran Holds Delayed State Funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei Amid Calls for Trump's Death
bullets: Massive funeral ceremony began for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed at age 86 in a February 28 US-Israel airstrike
The funeral was delayed nearly four months due to active war conditions and security concerns
A performer at the ceremony drew cheers asking 'Why is the most bastard man in the world still alive?' referring to Trump
Representatives from approximately 30 countries are attending, including delegations from India, with the ceremony expected to last six days

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of funeral proceedings and attendee list, including specific Indian delegation members

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Separate article focused on death threats against Trump at ceremony

top_stories.headline: French Court Verdict Tuesday Could End Marine Le Pen's Presidential Ambitions
bullets: Paris appeal court will rule Tuesday on Le Pen's conviction for misuse of European parliamentary funds
If the court upholds the initial verdict, Le Pen would be declared ineligible for public office, ending her 2027 presidential campaign
Current polls show the 57-year-old National Rally leader well-positioned to become France's next president
If barred, 30-year-old Jordan Bardella would automatically become the RN's candidate; polls suggest he would also be favored

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage emphasizing high political stakes and potential succession to Bardella
